description_long: This is a large, roughly hemispherical bowl with a wide opening and a visibly thick rim. The surface is matte and uneven, showing signs of age and wear, with a grayish-brown coloration and some lighter patches. The bowl is broken, with several large missing sections along the rim, exposing the thickness of the material. There are no visible handles or decorative patterns, but two small labels are affixed to one side, suggesting it may be cataloged or part of a collection. The interior is similarly rough and unadorned, with a slightly concave base. The overall appearance suggests it is made from fired clay or ceramic, likely hand-formed and utilitarian in nature.
description: A large, broken, grayish-brown ceramic bowl with a rough, matte surface and no visible decoration.
description_view_0: This view shows a prominent break in the rim and highlights the bowl's thick, rough-walled construction.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a wide, open mouth and a smoother curve on the bowl's body, with the break less pronounced.
description_view_2: This side displays two small labels attached to the exterior and a large missing section of the rim.
description_view_3: This perspective shows a more continuous rim with subtle surface discoloration and a less visible break.
attribute_context_of_use: holding or serving food or ingredients

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
